content.question: What is the minimum school-quality convergence (measured by per-student resourcing, teacher qualifications, outcome distributions by school) required before track-removal or admissions-system reform would produce improved equity outcomes rather than shifting segregation to other mechanisms (private schooling, residential sorting, parental tutoring)?
content.reduction_path: Map current between-school variance in per-student spending, staffing quality, and outcome distributions; model the relationship between school-quality gaps and parental track-seeking behavior using household-survey or school-choice data; pilot a school-equalization intervention in a matched pair of regions (one with parallel track removal, one without) to measure whether quality convergence reduces track-demand before central policy is extended.
